story.text: As an AI, I need to brief bdo on each open item well enough to judge it in under a minute — value first, mechanism after — on a page he can open from a laptop or phone, because bdo arrives blind or distracted and his stamp should be leverage, not homework.

briefing.headline: Approve the inbox you are reading right now
briefing.value: You asked: 'do you have a way of showing me my open items and a way for me to clear them?' This is that — one place showing everything waiting on you, each item briefed well enough to judge in under a minute without re-validating the whole project, readable from a laptop or phone.
briefing.why_now: Your stamp is the system's slowest and most valuable stage. v0 shipped as a terse CLI list and you amended it (rcp.efd40f7169c8): too thin to judge blind, not reachable from a phone. This v1 carries the web rendering and the richer briefing you asked for — and is the first atom told this way.
briefing.if_accepted: The web inbox becomes the owner surface; atoms are expected to carry a briefing like this one from now on; later versions grow it toward a served, authenticated page you can open anywhere.
briefing.if_rejected: The CLI list stays the only surface, the briefing expectation is dropped, and your queue stays terse — judging keeps costing you the 30 minutes you said you don't have.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: this changes views and the ticket schema only — no gate logic, no truth moved, no new write path. Reverting is deleting a renderer.
briefing.mechanism: A pure fold over atoms/ + log/ renders a self-contained inbox.html (works from disk, as an artifact, or on any static host); 'python -m loop.web serve' serves it locally with verdict buttons that write through the one existing pen (node judge). Localhost-only until auth is built.

_note: This file is the atom's birth record and content-hash source. It is never mutated: current verdicts and state are always the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5). v0's history stays on the log; this version answers its amend receipt.
